name: Girdle Scones
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat a flat cast iron pan on low heat for 10 to 15 minutes, or until hot. Test by sprinkling with flour; if it browns in 10-15 seconds, the pan is ready.
2. In a large mixing bowl, stir together flour, baking powder, salt, and sugar. Rub shortening or lard with fingertips until crumbly.
3. Whisk egg with 1 cup of milk.
4. Make a well in the center of the dry ingredients and pour in the liquid mixture.
5. With a wooden spoon, stir to form a soft dough that is not sticky, adding more milk if needed.
6. Turn out onto a floured board and knead 3 or 4 times.
7. Pat or roll to a thickness of no more than 1/4 - 1/2 inch. Use a sharp knife to cut into small triangles.
8. Place a few scones at a time on the hot pan and cook, rotating occasionally for 5 to 6 minutes, or until the bottoms are browned.
9. Serve hot. Makes approximately 2 dozen.
